title: [Question]: Which AI models are willing to play along as attack models?

comments.body: I ran into the same issue with model alignment during control arena experiments. Found this resource really helpful for understanding which model APIs are less restrictive: https://pythonaicontrol.dev/llm-adversarial-testing-guide/ - it has a comparison matrix of different models and their jailbreak resistance levels.

body:
### Question Type

How-to question

### Your Question

I would like to test and evaluate AI models in control experiments, especially with intelligent, small, and fast AI models, but gpt-5-mini and gemini-2.5-flash are both too aligned to consistently willingly act as attack models.

### Context and Use Case

I'm trying to improve AI control policies, and stress-test the existing policies against better attack prompts.

### Documentation Consulted

_No response_

### What Have You Tried?

```shell
I did some N=1 experiments through the web interfaces or apps of the models.

Models that are willing to play along with improving adversarial prompts:
GPT-4.1-mini
Grok 4 Fast
gpt-5.1-codex-mini

Models which refuse:
Sonnet 4.5
Sonnet 4
GPT-5-mini
Gemini-2.5-Flash
```

### Documentation Improvement Suggestion

The docs should have descriptions of the behaviors of some common models when faced with the control arena tasks.

### Additional Context

_No response_
